perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto. Revocation requires a signed writing but notification to other parties is optional and does not affect the revocation's validity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the revocation process. The original text clearly states revocation requires written notification to each party for the revocation to take effect. The altered text makes notification optional, creating an in-text contradiction because the original statement enforces mandatory notification, while the modified text implies optional notification. This contradiction becomes apparent if one party revokes the agreement without notifying the others, leading to confusion about the agreement's validity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each party hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto. This agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto. Revocation requires a signed writing but notification to other parties is optional and does not affect the revocation's validity. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto, being duly authorized, has caused this agreement to be executed and effective as of the date set forth below. Date: April 7, 2020 BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION By: Ally Pecarro Name: Ally Pecarro Title: Attorney-in-fact BANC OF AMERICA PREFERRED FUNDING CORPORATION By: Michael Jentis Name: Michael Jentis Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the revocation process, stating that notification to other parties is optional. This directly contradicts the original requirement that revocation requires written notification to each party for it to take effect. As the change introducing optionality of notification exists in the same 'Contradicted Text', and the original text required notification to each other party, there now exists two conflicting in-text methods for revocation.

perturbation.original_text: JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each party hereto hereby agrees to the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each party hereto agrees to cooperate in the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each party is responsible for their individual filings; joint filings are for convenience and information only.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'agrees to the joint filing' with 'agrees to cooperate in the joint filing,' and adds a sentence clarifying 'joint filings are for convenience and information only.' This introduces an in-text contradiction because the first part of the original text, indicates an agreement to file jointly which entails shared responsibility. Now with the changed text, it states it is just for convenience, contradicting the initial agreement to 'cooperate' in the joint filing by diminishing its binding nature and implies individual filings still need to occur. This makes the initial commitment to joint filings unenforceable as parties can claim they only 'cooperated' but didn't commit to the joint filing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: JOINT FILING AGREEMENT Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), and the rules and regulations thereunder, each party hereto agrees to cooperate in the joint filing, on behalf of each of them, of any filing required by such party under Section 13 or Section 16 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (and, if such security is registered on a national securities exchange, also with the exchange), and further agrees to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each party is responsible for their individual filings; joint filings are for convenience and information only. This agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto, being duly authorized, has caused this agreement to be executed and effective as of the date set forth below. Date: April 7, 2020 BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION By: Ally Pecarro Name: Ally Pecarro Title: Attorney-in-fact BANC OF AMERICA PREFERRED FUNDING CORPORATION By: Michael Jentis Name: Michael Jentis Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'Each party is responsible for their individual filings; joint filings are for convenience and information only.' This directly contradicts the initial agreement to cooperate in the joint filing found in both the changed text and the contradicted text. The original text implies a binding agreement for joint filing, while the changed text diminishes its binding nature by stating it is for convenience only and implying individual filings are still necessary, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto, being duly authorized, has caused this agreement to be executed and effective as of the date set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto intends this agreement to become effective as of the date set forth below, subject to any further internal approvals required by their respective organizations.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a condition for the agreement's effectiveness. The original text states the agreement is effective as of the date specified. The modified text adds a caveat: the agreement's effectiveness is 'subject to any further internal approvals.' This contradicts the direct assertion of effectiveness in the original text. It makes it unclear whether the signatures alone bind the parties, or if additional, unspecified approvals are necessary, creating an in-text contradiction to whether signatures alone are adequate for effectiveness of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each party hereto intends this agreement to become effective as of the date set forth below, subject to any further internal approvals required by their respective organizations. Date: April 7, 2020 BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION By: Ally Pecarro Name: Ally Pecarro Title: Attorney-in-fact BANC OF AMERICA PREFERRED FUNDING CORPORATION By: Michael Jentis Name: Michael Jentis Title: Authorized Signatory
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'subject to any further internal approvals' which directly contradicts the intent that the agreement becomes effective as of the date set forth. The added conditionality makes the effective date uncertain. The contradictory language concerning internal approvals creates ambiguity, thereby causing a direct contradiction with other sections that rely on a definitive start date.
